questions.question: By the late 1960s, what major achievement did India attain in agriculture?
questions.solution: By the late 1960s, India had achieved self-sufficiency in food grains. This was mainly due to the Green Revolution which increased agricultural production. India stopped depending heavily on food imports and met its internal demand. Hence, the correct achievement is becoming self-sufficient in food grains. Final answer: Option 4

questions.question: Social indicators of development improved after economic growth accelerated. Which of the following indicators saw the most consistent improvement after 1991?
questions.solution: Correct option is 3: Maternal Mortality Rate. After 1991, India experienced economic growth which positively impacted social indicators. Among the given options: - Fiscal deficit is an economic measure, often fluctuating with policy changes. - Consumer Price Index reflects inflation, which can vary independently of development. - Maternal Mortality Rate consistently improved due to better healthcare and social policies following economic reforms. - Gini coefficient measures income inequality, which did not show consistent improvement. Thus, the Maternal Mortality Rate shows the most consistent improvement after 1991 reforms. Final answer: Option 3

questions.question: The 'Middle-Income Trap' refers to ____________.
questions.option_2: Struggle to attain high income status
questions.solution: The 'Middle-Income Trap' is an economic condition. It describes the difficulty countries face after achieving middle-income levels but fail to progress to high-income status. Countries struggle to sustain growth and upgrade industries or move up the value chain. Hence, the trap is the struggle to reach high income status after initial growth. Option 2 is correct.

questions.question: Under the Stand-Up India Scheme, bank loans from Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) are provided in what range for eligible beneficiaries?
questions.solution: Solution: Under the Stand-Up India Scheme , loans are provided by Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) . The scheme supports SC/ST and women entrepreneurs . The loan range is fixed between ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore .

questions.question: What is it called when the government sells part of its share in public sector companies to the public?
questions.solution: The process of the government selling its stake in public sector companies to the public is known as Disinvestment. This reduces the government’s shareholding in these companies. It is done to improve efficiency and raise funds. Hence, the correct term is Disinvestment (Option 3). Answer complete.

questions.question: Which economic model advocates that developing countries should prioritize import substitution over free trade to promote economic development?
questions.solution: Option 4 is correct. Import Substitution Industrialization (ISI) is an economic strategy that encourages developing countries to reduce dependency on imports by producing goods domestically. This model promotes substituting imports with local production to foster economic development. Unlike free trade models, ISI protects local industries through tariffs and quotas. Therefore, ISI advocates prioritising import substitution over free trade for development. Hence, the answer is Import Substitution Industrialization.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct about a non-Constitutional body, Reserve Bank of India (RBI)? (1) RBI performs several crucial functions like regulating and supervising the banking system, managing the currency and credit system and managing foreign exchange reserves. (2) RBI was established on April 1, 1935 in accordance with the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. (3) The Central Office of the RBI was initially established in Kolkata but was permanently moved to Mumbai in 1937
questions.solution: All the given statements about the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) are correct. Statement (1): RBI does perform crucial functions such as regulating and supervising the banking system, managing currency and credit, and managing foreign exchange reserves. Statement (2): RBI was established on 1st April 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. Statement (3): The central office of the RBI was initially in Kolkata and was permanently moved to Mumbai in 1937. Hence, the correct answer is option 4, as all three statements are true.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the Namo Drone Didi scheme is/are correct? 1. It is a Central Sector Scheme for providing drones to Women Self Help Groups for the period from 2024-25 to 2025-26 with an outlay of ₹1,261 crore. 2. The scheme aims to provide drones to 15,000 selected Women Self Help Groups for providing rental services to farmers for agriculture purposes. 3. Under the scheme, Women Self Help Groups receive substantial financial assistance, covering 80% of the drone and accessory costs, up to ₹8 lakh.
questions.solution: The Namo Drone Didi scheme is a Central Sector Scheme running from 2024-25 to 2025-26. It has an outlay of ₹1,261 crore to provide drones to Women Self Help Groups (WSHGs). The scheme targets 15,000 selected WSHGs to offer drone rental services for agriculture. It provides up to 80% financial assistance on drone and accessory costs, capped at ₹8 lakh. All three statements match the official details of the Namo Drone Didi scheme. Hence, statements 1, 2 and 3 are correct. Final answer: Option 3

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT a major recommendation of the Kelkar Task Force (2002) on direct taxes?
questions.option_1: Elimination of the minimum alternate tax on corporate entities
questions.option_2: Elimination of the standard deduction for salaried taxpayers
questions.option_3: Reduction in corporate tax rate to 40 percent for domestic companies
questions.option_4: Extension of PAN to cover all economic agents/citizens
questions.solution: The Kelkar Task Force (2002) recommended measures to rationalise and simplify direct taxes in India. It suggested the elimination of minimum alternate tax (MAT) on corporate entities to reduce tax litigation and improve tax system fairness. It also recommended removal of the standard deduction for salaried taxpayers to widen the tax base and simplify calculations. Extension of PAN (Permanent Account Number) to all economic agents/citizens was advised to improve tax compliance and tracking. However, it did NOT recommend reducing the corporate tax rate to 40% for domestic companies; this was not part of their major recommendations. Hence, Option 3 is the correct answer as the recommendation that was NOT made by the Kelkar Task Force.
